title: Claw Sharpening Techniques for Spring
description: A guide to keeping your cat's claws healthy and sharp during the active spring season.
main_content: Spring brings more outdoor activity for cats, making claw maintenance crucial. Check claws weekly for splits or overgrowth. Provide scratching posts with varying textures like sisal and cardboard. For indoor cats, trim claws every 2-3 weeks using proper clippers. Reward with treats after trimming. Consider claw caps for furniture protection. Wild cats naturally sharpen on trees; mimic this with vertical scratching surfaces.
key_takeaways: Trim indoor cat claws every 2-3 weeks
Use positive reinforcement during claw care
